archimate:ApplicationService.name: Big Data Analytics
archimate:ApplicationService.documentation:
Definition: A Big Data Analytics Application service refers to a software application or platform that leverages advanced analytics techniques to process and analyze large and complex datasets, commonly known as big data. It enables organizations to derive valuable insights, identify patterns, trends, and correlations, and make data-driven decisions. The service typically includes data ingestion, storage, processing, and visualization capabilities, as well as advanced analytics techniques such as machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive modeling.
Source: NIST
LOST view: TVA-Analytics Enablers [Motivation]
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/BigDataAnalyticsApplicationService
ABB name: egovera:BigDataAnalyticsApplicationService
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/BigDataAnalyticsApplicationService
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataAnalyticsApplicationService

properties.value: A Big Data Analytics Application service refers to a software application or platform that leverages advanced analytics techniques to process and analyze large and complex datasets, commonly known as big data. It enables organizations to derive valuable insights, identify patterns, trends, and correlations, and make data-driven decisions. The service typically includes data ingestion, storage, processing, and visualization capabilities, as well as advanced analytics techniques such as machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive modeling.
